evals.prompt: Give me a branch name for the docs update I just made to the install guide.
evals.expected_output: A single branch name, with no surrounding explanation.
expectations: The skill classifies this as an output workflow.
The skill returns only the branch name.
The branch name follows the branch naming convention.

evals.prompt: Create a branch for this bugfix. The login form redirects to /home even when auth fails.
evals.expected_output: A Git branch is created or a concise blocker is reported.
expectations: The skill classifies this as an action workflow.
The skill uses the branch naming reference.
The skill avoids extra narrative on successful branch creation.

evals.prompt: Write a commit message for the staged changes.
evals.expected_output: A single Conventional Commits message, with no surrounding explanation.
expectations: The skill classifies this as an output workflow.
The skill uses the commit message reference.
The skill prefers git message when staged changes are the source material.

evals.prompt: Commit the staged changes using our normal commit message format.
evals.expected_output: The staged changes are committed or a concise blocker is reported.
expectations: The skill classifies this as an action workflow.
The skill commits staged changes only.
The skill avoids extra narrative on successful commit.

evals.prompt: Review this branch name: feat/login fixes
evals.expected_output: Concise branch-name review feedback with a corrected branch name if needed.
expectations: The skill classifies this as an output workflow.
The skill uses the branch naming reference.
The response identifies spaces or malformed structure as a problem.
The response provides a valid replacement branch name.

evals.prompt: Start a hotfix branch for a production payment webhook signature failure.
evals.expected_output: A hotfix branch is created or a concise blocker is reported.
expectations: The skill classifies this as an action workflow.
The skill inspects current branch/worktree before branch action.
The branch name reflects hotfix/payment webhook signature context.
The response avoids unnecessary explanation on success.

evals.prompt: Improve this commit message: 'stuff'. The staged diff updates README setup instructions and fixes a typo in the API docs.
evals.expected_output: A Conventional Commits docs message with concise wording and no body unless needed.
expectations: The skill classifies this as an output workflow.
The skill uses the commit message reference.
The message uses the docs type.
The summary is imperative and has no trailing period.

evals.prompt: Commit everything in the working tree.
evals.expected_output: A concise blocker or clarification because the skill should commit staged changes only and not stage unrelated files by default.
expectations: The skill does not stage unstaged files by default.
The skill reports that committing uses staged changes only.
The skill asks at most one focused question if no staged changes exist.
The skill does not run destructive git commands.
